title: ‘Scripted conspiracy by Bajrang Dal’: Uncle of accused in TCS Nashik case says 'not a major scandal'
content: Two accused in the Tata Consultancy Services Nashik sexual harassment case remain in police custody. An accused relative claims the allegations are part of a conspiracy and scripted. Police have arrested seven employees so far. A Special Investigation Team is probing complaints from eight women. Tata Consultancy Services has a zero-tolerance policy and is investigating the matter.

title: 'Since no one is complementing ... ': Shreyas Iyer's cheeky take after breathtaking Hardik Pandya catch - WATCH
content: Shreyas Iyer's spectacular mid-air relay catch wowed fans and drew viral reactions from teammates. Despite a strong century from Quinton de Kock, Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh's consecutive half-centuries powered Punjab Kings to a seven-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, propelling PBKS to the top of the table.

title: Melinda Gates on 'one simple rule' that she made sure her children follow while growing up
content: Melinda French Gates instilled a core principle in her children: "Just because we can doesn’t mean we should." This guided them to value their own achievements over inherited wealth, fostering empathy and independence.  Her approach aimed to prevent privilege from alienating them from themselves and others.

title: IPL 2026: Amid Sooryavanshi and Arya noise, Prabhsimran Singh is quietly 'raising the bar'
content: Prabhsimran Singh has been part of Punjab Kings, earlier Kings XI Punjab, since 2019, when the IPL franchise splurged Rs 4.8 crore on the youngster. Over the last few seasons, the hard-hitting batter has found consistency, with Ricky Ponting playing a big role in it.

title: Passport row: SC refuses relief to Pawan Khera, declines plea to extend transit bail
content: The Supreme Court denied Congress leader Pawan Khera's request for extended transit anticipatory bail, impacting his efforts to secure protection before an Assam court. This follows the apex court's stay on a Telangana High Court order, raising concerns about "forum shopping" as the FIR originated in Assam. Khera can still seek bail in Assam.

title: 'Gift wrapped in barbed wire': Tharoor slams Centre for linking women’s quota with Delimitation Bill
content: Senior Congress leader Shashi Tharoor criticized the proposed delimitation exercise as "political demonetisation," arguing that linking women's reservation to constituency redrawing and census data unnecessarily complicates a widely supported reform. He urged immediate implementation of women's reservation, warning that delimitation could disrupt federalism and political power balances.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' fails to break 'Dhurandhar' record for highest week 4 collection
content: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' continues its impressive box office journey, crossing Rs 1,100 crore in India and setting a new record for fourth-week collections. While earnings dipped on its 29th day, the film's overall performance remains stellar, securing the second-highest Week 4 earnings ever. Its global gross now stands at an astounding Rs 1,737.74 crore.

title: 'False information': Vinod Kambli's wife Andrea Hewitt hits back at health rumours
content: Reports about Vinod Kambli's deteriorating health, including claims of a brain clot affecting his memory, have circulated widely. However, his wife Andrea Hewitt has dismissed these as false, stating he is fine. While Kambli has been seen using a walking stick, his family asserts he is recovering.

title: Tesla CEO Elon Musk: This is 'best way’ to deal with job losses caused by AI
content: Elon Musk has again proposed universal high income, suggesting government checks could offset AI-driven job losses. He believes this won't cause inflation, as AI-produced goods will outpace money supply increases. Musk envisions a future free from poverty, where saving money becomes unnecessary due to ample income. However, his ideas have drawn skepticism regarding dependency and fairness.

title: Jacqueline Fernandez seeks to turn approver in Rs 200 crore Sukesh Chandrashekhar case
content: Bollywood actress Jacqueline Fernandez has filed a plea to become an approver in the Rs 200 crore money-laundering case involving Sukesh Chandrashekhar. The Enforcement Directorate is investigating the alleged extortion racket. Fernandez, who has been questioned previously, has now expressed her willingness to cooperate with the agency by turning approver in the high-profile case.

title: Rohit Shetty house firing: UP STF & Mumbai Police arrest 9th accused in Agra - Report
content: In a significant development, Pradeep Kumar, also known as Gaath, was apprehended in Agra by the Uttar Pradesh STF in collaboration with the Mumbai Police Crime Branch. Kumar has been identified as a pivotal member of the Shubham Lonkar gang, implicated in the shooting incident at filmmaker Rohit Shetty's residence in Mumbai. Read on to know more in detail.

title: How drones deployed from aircraft, helicopters could change aerial warfare
content: India's Air Force is enhancing its offensive capabilities by seeking drones deployable from helicopters and loitering munitions from transport aircraft. These advanced systems, capable of various missions including electronic warfare and target acquisition, aim to free up fighter jets and counter adversaries effectively. Similar initiatives are underway globally, highlighting a significant shift in future warfare strategies.

title: India's longest range electric scooter: Simple Ultra launched with 400 km range!
content: Bengaluru-based Simple Energy has launched the Simple Ultra, India's longest-range electric scooter with a claimed 400 km IDC range. Priced at Rs 2.35 lakh, the scooter boasts a 6.5 kWh battery, a top speed of 115 kmph, and a 0-40 kmph acceleration of 2.77 seconds.

title: Shredded to cosmic dust: Stunning footage captures comet’s death plunge into the Sun — watch
content: Indian space enthusiasts, brace yourselves! Nasa spacecraft have witnessed the dramatic demise of comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) as it ventured too close to the Sun. Observations from SOHO, STEREO, and PUNCH revealed the icy wanderer disintegrating into a dust cloud, likely hours before its closest solar approach.

title: Army Commander's conference concludes, lessons from ongoing conflicts discussed
content: In a landmark gathering, India's foremost military leaders came together for a three-day Army Commander's Conference dedicated to transforming the armed forces into a cutting-edge 'Future Ready Force'. The agenda was rich with strategic discussions on integrating advanced technologies, achieving robust networking capabilities by 2026, advancing unmanned systems, and extracting valuable insights from current global conflicts.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 9 result 2026 released: Direct link to download scorecards
content: The Jharkhand Academic Council has announced the Class 9 Result 2026. Students can check their scores online on the official JAC website. Results are also available via SMS and DigiLocker. The examinations were held on March 6 and 7, 2026. Over 4 lakh students appeared for the exam. The board evaluated OMR sheets and internal assessments for the results.

title: Telangana: 12 lakh people prefer ‘No Caste’ tag in survey, most in Greater Hyderabad
content: Nearly 12 lakh people identified as 'No Caste' in Telangana's SEEEPC survey, representing 3.4% of the population. This group, largely urban and educated, exhibits higher income, employment in professional jobs, and better asset ownership, indicating a detachment from caste's negative consequences.

title: Amaravati sex abuse case: Mastermind teen Ayaan Ahmed casual confession stuns cops
content: Amravati sex abuse and blackmail case mastermind Ayaan Ahmed reportedly told sleuths he became a 'playboy' after his first breakup. Investigations reveal he used earnings from his cellphone business and Instagram promotions to fund a promiscuous lifestyle, luring women online over several months before allegedly coercing them into sexual favors.

title: Niggle behind slump? Jayawardene on Bumrah's struggling form
content: Star pacer Jasprit Bumrah is enduring a rare wicketless streak in IPL 2026, going five matches without success. Despite this, coach Mahela Jayawardene backs his bowler, citing a minor World Cup niggle and a need for luck.  The Mumbai Indians bowling unit, however, admits to lacking penetration this season, contributing to their current ninth-place standing.

title: India’s big nuclear leap: How Stage 2 criticality will power the road to energy security
content: India's nuclear ambitions have reached a pivotal stage with the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor achieving criticality, marking a significant step in its three-stage nuclear program. This advancement positions India as a leader in advanced nuclear technology, bolstering its drive for energy self-reliance and security amidst a rapidly expanding power sector.

title: Virat Kohli’s instagram 'like' sparks meme storm, fans revive 'Algorithm' joke
content: Virat Kohli's recent Instagram 'like' on a post by influencer LizLaz has sparked a new wave of memes and online discussion. This incident, similar to a past one involving Avneet Kaur, has fans humorously blaming the 'algorithm.' Teammate Yuzvendra Chahal's subsequent comment on the post has also gone viral.

title: Interior design: 5 decor styling tricks that make any home look expensive without renovating
content: Interior experts reveal five budget-friendly hacks to elevate your home's aesthetic without costly renovations. Simple changes like choosing the right paint colours, upgrading lighting, decluttering, layering textures and adding statement pieces sparingly can instantly create a more luxurious feel. Focusing on intentionality and editing rather than adding is key to achieving a high-end look.

title: Supply shortage of gold, silver? Banks stop import orders as metals stuck at customs
content: Typically, the Directorate General of Foreign Trade, operating under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, issues a notification at the beginning of each financial year specifying the banks authorised by the Reserve Bank of India to import gold and silver.

title: The air you breathe is making you sick earlier than you think, finds UK study
content: A new UK study reveals that air pollution significantly accelerates the onset of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, digestive, and cancer categories.  Researchers found that higher exposure to particulate matter and nitrogen oxides leads to earlier diagnoses, potentially shaving years off healthy life expectancy. The findings highlight the urgent need for improved air quality to slow disease progression.

title: 'Building castles in air': Iran mocks Trump’s 'will get free oil, free Hormuz' claim
content: Iran's state media has dismissed US President Donald Trump's claims of a negotiation breakthrough as "building castles in the air." Trump stated that Iran is close to a deal and has agreed to hand over its enriched uranium, a key point of contention in the ongoing conflict.

title: 'We don’t want to push': Big Rohit Sharma update from MI head coach
content: Rohit Sharma was rested for Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings due to a hamstring injury. Despite his absence, head coach Mahela Jayawardene stated it's not a serious issue and the team's struggles are a collective responsibility. Mumbai Indians suffered their fourth consecutive defeat, falling to ninth place in the standings.

title: Nitish Kumar Reddy Interview: ‘I have overdone things… now I realise that’
content: In an exclusive conversation with TimesofIndia.com, the all-rounder opens up on the need of not overdoing things, the mental toughness, awareness about his game and the dream of playing for India in 2027 ODI World Cup.

title: Wipro shares slide up to 4% after weak Q4, muted outlook dents sentiment
content: Wipro's shares tumbled as profit dipped and the IT firm projected a subdued outlook, citing cautious client spending.  Revenue growth in its core IT services remained sluggish, raising concerns about persistent challenges.  Despite a share buyback, weak guidance and margin pressures overshadowed positive deal bookings, leading to a significant year-to-date stock decline.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' leak: Madras HC blocks piracy; cybercrime probe makes arrests
content: Thalapathy Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' faced a major leak before its release, with initial clips followed by a full movie download. Following widespread condemnation and legal action, the Madras High Court ordered a ban on illegal circulation. Police investigations revealed the leak originated from the editing room, leading to arrests and a warning against piracy.

title: Delimitation: Between constitutional statesmanship and political expediency
content: Parliament considers crucial delimitation bills, raising concerns for democratic legitimacy and India's federal balance. These proposals could alter political power distribution significantly. The article argues for careful deliberation and public consultation before any constitutional changes are enacted. Statesmanship demands scrutiny, not speed, to ensure fairness and preserve the Union's integrity.

title: Fresh workers’ protest erupts in Noida over salary hike demand, police deployed
content: Fresh protests erupted in Noida over salary hikes, while police revealed social media accounts from Pakistan allegedly fueled earlier labor unrest. Investigations point to a coordinated effort involving digital misinformation and on-ground mobilization by groups like Mazdoor Bigul Dasta, with several arrests made and more accused sought.

title: 0 wickets in 5 matches! What's really going wrong with Bumrah? - Explained
content: Star pacer Jasprit Bumrah is struggling in IPL 2026, having conceded 164 runs without a wicket in five matches. Former India cricketer Irfan Pathan suggests Bumrah is bowling too many slower deliveries, with his average speed dropping below 130 kmph. Pathan believes reducing the percentage of slower balls and increasing pace will improve his effectiveness.

title: Asian stocks today: Stocks ease but set for weekly gains amid optimism on war de-escalation
content: Asian markets saw a slight dip Friday after a strong rally, as investors braced for a crucial weekend potentially impacting the Middle East conflict. Despite ceasefire hopes and easing oil prices, caution persists. Wall Street's record highs offered some support, while currency markets reflected reduced risk aversion, though the Strait of Hormuz closure remains a concern.

title: How all 32 teams can crush the NFL draft: We have prospects and positions to target
content: A week before the draft, Ben Solak hits the top priorities for every single team -- and the prospects who can meet those needs.

title: Sources: Collier back to Lynx on supermax deal
content: Napheesa Collier has signed a one-year, $1.4 million supermax contract to return to Minnesota, sources said.

title: McDavid seals 6th scoring title with 4-assist finale
content: The Oilers' Connor McDavid won his sixth Art Ross title as the NHL scoring leader, finishing the regular season with 138 points after Thursday's four-assist performance in a 6-1 win over the Canucks.

title: Messick loses no-hit bid in 9th; Guardians hold on
content: Guardians rookie Parker Messick's bid for MLB's first no-hitter of 2026 was broken up in the ninth inning, but Cleveland held on for a 4-2 win over the Orioles on Thursday night.

title: Trout's latest blast caps 5-homer series in Bronx
content: Mike Trout homered for the fifth time during a four-game series at Yankee Stadium by crushing a 446-foot drive in the seventh inning.

title: Devils hire Panthers executive Mehta as GM
content: The Devils have hired Sunny Mehta, who was an assistant GM when the Panthers won the Stanley Cup the past two years, as their general manager.

title: WR Knox found negligent in crash, to pay $2.8M
content: A Texas court on Wednesday issued a default judgment of more than $2.8 million against Theodore Knox, the co-defendant in one of the civil lawsuits against Chiefs receiver Rashee Rice stemming from a 2024 street racing crash in Dallas.

title: OU's Torrez stuns by taking NCAA all-around title
content: Oklahoma's Faith Torrez made her all-around season debut Thursday night and defeated Olympic and world champions to claim NCAA gymnastics' highest individual honor.

title: Sources: NWSL expected to vote on calendar shift
content: The NWSL's board of governors is expected to vote later this month on whether to flip the league's calendar to a fall-to-spring season, multiple sources told ESPN.

content: You know about the reveal mishap. Here's what went into creating the sculpture that will forever honor a Seattle icon.

content: The LA Clippers saw their season end Wednesday, and an ex-Clipper made sure to rub salt in the wound.

title: 'A bigger shock to the system than we acknowledged': How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
content: After a devastating blowout loss in Oklahoma City on April 2, the Lakers lost two of their three stars. Now they face Kevin Durant and the Houston Rockets -- as a home underdog.

content: Fifita, who runs monthly community events, is one of just five senior QBs nationally who has not transferred.

title: Semifinal predictions: Don't bet against Arsenal despite misfiring attack
content: The UEFA Champions League quarterfinals largely lived up to expectations, with three of four games going down to the wire. Now that the dust has settled, let's break down the semifinals and predict who advances.

title: LIV Golf is still going, but its days seem numbered and probably always were
content: LIV made a lot of noise, caused some nervous days at the PGA Tour and even forced some positive changes. But ultimately, it never did enough to make a real challenge.

title: MLB Power Rankings: NL teams dominate top 10 with two debuts
content: Three weeks into the season, two red-hot NL teams are shooting up our list while another powerhouse plummets.

title: Women's college basketball transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: Iowa-bound Dani Carnegie moves up to No. 4. Jada Williams, who is transferring to LSU, jumps to No. 6.

title: Verstappen finding joy in his Nürburgring side quest
content: Max Verstappen hasn't been very happy in F1 this year, but his Nürburgring 24 Hours side quest is reinvigorating the four-time world champion.

content: NEC have the most attacking, aggressive style around, and it's working as they sit third in the Dutch Eredivisie and are into the final of the Dutch Cup.

content: Just back from injury, Alexander Isak has to hit the ground running if Liverpool are to reach the Champions League.

content: Tottenham Hotspur midfielder Archie Gray is being considered for a summer move by Bayern Munich and Liverpool. Transfer Talk has the latest.

content: An early setback and a positive test -- since cleared -- didn't define Baumgardner. Now, she's on the doorstep of leading the sport.

content: A case has been registered under relevant sections of the Indian Penal Code and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act. The man was produced before a court and remanded to judicial custody.

content: TVK's manifesto goes beyond MSP, promising accident insurance, a fishermen protection rights fund and large-scale coastal infrastructure, including sea erosion barriers stretching up to 200 km in vulnerable districts.

content: The move reflects San Diego's shift from years of drought-driven conservation to a position of relative abundance.

content: Seven employees of the TCS' BPO unit in Nashik have been arrested after they were accused of sexual harassment and forcible religious conversion.

content: Countries in East Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa are among the countries most affected now -- and who could suffer the most -- outside the region, as the conflict stretches on.

content: The initiative aims to support ongoing anti-crime campaigns like 'Gangstran Te Vaar' and the anti-drug drive 'Yudh Nashiyan Virudh'.

title: Is Earth’s orbit getting too crowded? 1.5L collision alerts for Indian satellites in 2025
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More

title: ‘Fascist BJP regime against Tamil, Tamil Nadu and its rights,’ says Udhayanidhi
content: Udhayanidhi Stalin says there is a threat to the Tamil language, Tamil Nadu and its Rights by the fascist BJP regime; this Assembly election was to protect our rights and the people of Madurai know this

title: Parliament special sitting LIVE: 'Bill has nothing to do with women empowerment; trying to bypass caste census,' Rahul Gandhi says
content: Lok Sabha to vote on three key bills at 4 p.m.; PM Narendra Modi and Amit Shah told the House that the Bills to amend the women’s reservation law and allow delimitation exercise will keep current proportional representation of southern States intact

title: Actor Allu Arjun moves Delhi High Court for protection of personality rights
content: Counsel refers to 'fake-call Pushpa' app; Justice Tushar Rao Gedela indicates that the court would pass an interim order in the Telugu actor’ favour

title: Kerala BDS student death: Two accused faculty members move court for anticipatory bail
content: Dr. M.K. Ram and Dr. Sangeeta Nambiar, who remain absconding, contend in their bail plea that they had no role in Nithin Raj’s death and argue that the student was under stress due to threats from a loan app

title: Six persons, including three women from single family, burnt alive in road accident near Shorapur in Yadgir district of Karnataka
content: Initial reports suggest that the victims were returning to Sirwar after visiting Sri Venugopal Swamy temple in Shorapur

title: Telangana ACB arrests Excise Inspector while accepting ₹2 Lakh bribe in Kamareddy
content: ACB appealed to public to report instances of bribery by public servants through its toll-free number 1064

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: VCK pulling out all stops to keep PMK at bay in Kattumannarkoil contest
content: While the VCK is seeking to consolidate its dominance, the PMK is looking to rewire the caste equations in the northern districts. The contest looks evenly balanced, with Adi Dravidar and Muslim voters aligning with the DMK alliance, and Vanniyars consolidating behind the AIADMK front

title: Kerala elections: Kozhikode MP M.K. Raghavan slams Congress leaders’ public spat over CM candidate

content: While the word was coined in the U.S., it has been frequently used across the globe, including France and the U.K.

content: There searches are said to be a result of intelligence reports that gifts, meant for distribution to voters, were stored in these places

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin’s assets: Plea in Madras High Court seeks I-T department probe
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan slated to hear the case, filed by a voter from Chepauk-Triplicane constituency, on Wednesday

title: AI memes on social media catch Gen Z’s eye this election in Tamil Nadu
content: There was a time when only a few media houses came up with animations of political candidates during the election season, but now, almost anyone can create them with the help of AI

title: SC allows Pawan Khera to seek anticipatory bail in Assam court, declines interim protection

title: Delimitation will turn out to be 'political demonetisation': Shashi Tharoor in Lok Sabha
content: Women's reservation, he said, is ready for harvest and can and should be implemented immediately based on existing parliamentary strength

title: YSRCP and TDP spar over Juvvaladinne Fishing Harbour visit in SPSR Nellore district

title: Ashok Kharat case: ED conducts fresh raids in Maharashtra’s Nashik
content: ED officials said Kharat opened multiple bank accounts with two cooperative credit societies located in Nashik district in the name of third parties

title: NIT Kurukshetra student ends life; fourth case in two months triggers protests
content: According to the Adarsh Police Station SHO, the deceased Diksha Dubey hailed from Bihar and was pursuing B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science

content: Mr. Harivansh’s return to the post, marks the first time a nominated member holds the office of Deputy Chairman

content: ‘Didn’t you remember Goddess Kali?’ she said, taking a swipe at PM Modi’s ‘kaala teeka’ remark over DMK MPs wearing black-coloured clothes

content: CM Chandrababu Naidu directs officials to build 5,000 temples in two years using Srivani funds and prepare temple town plans

content: Ahead of Earth Day on April 22, we look at collectives in Ernakulam that are driving change through sustained, grassroots action

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: party manifestos
content: This is a compilation of poll manifestos released by prominent political parties in Tamil Nadu ahead of the State Assembly election scheduled to be held on April 23, 2026

title: Safeguard for States with stable populations is being removed with immediate effect
content: Bills seek to start the reallocation of seats and delimitation immediately, without 2026 sunset proviso

title: Congress accuses Centre of using delimitation as ‘political weapon’, urges delinking of women’s reservation law from delimitation
content: ‘What they did through delimitation in Jammu and Kashmir and in Assam, they want to do in the whole country in the name of women’s reservation,’ Congress leader Gaurav Gogoi says in Parliament, adding that the government’s actions were ‘shameful’

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Vijay releases TVK manifesto; criticises Dravidian majors’ poll promises
content: He asserts that his party has only promised what was possible, practical, and implementable if voted to power

title: Silence in legislations on maintaining proportionality makes TDP reach out to Home Minister, Law Minister
content: TDP wanted necessary amendments to clear the ambiguity, but with Home Minister Shah’s assurance in Lok Sabha on the issue, the party unlikely to press the issue further

title: Thousands hit streets in Imphal in torch rally over children’s killing, clash with forces
content: Thousands carrying torches began the rally around 7 p.m. on Thursday (April 16, 2026) at Singjamei, demanding the immediate arrest of those responsible for the April 7 blast at Tronglaobi in Bishnupur district that claimed the lives of two children

title: Supreme Court warns M.P. govt. of paramilitary deployment to rein in Chambal sand miners
content: The top court directed the installation of CCTV cameras, GPS tracking along the routes; matter posted for May 11

title: Income Tax raids rock poll bound West Bengal
content: The raids triggered not only sharp political reactions from the Trinamool Congress but party workers gathered near the sites where the raids were held and raised slogans against the BJP

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Distribution of booth slips under way
content: The Voter Information Slip contains voter’s details, location of the polling station, date and time of polling and a snapshot of the polling station map along with a list of Do’s and Don’ts to guide the voters on poll day, an official release said.

title: Delimitation Bill will not affect Tamil Nadu: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami
content: When Union Minister Amit Shah visited Tamil Nadu, he assured that the State will not be affected, stated Mr. Palaniswami. “The NDA will implement delimitation better than what Mr. Stalin expects. The Centre is clear that no State will be affected by the delimitation,” he added.

title: Representation of southern States will not go down, a misconception is being spread: Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah says “a major narrative is being built... a misconception is being spread” that the southern States will lose seats in the Lok Sabha due to delimitation

title: Nitish Kumar plans to groom his son Nishant before giving him big responsibility
content: Not allowing Nishant Kumar to be a part of Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary’s Cabinet is the larger political plan of former Chief Minister Nitish Kumar

title: Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan to visit Sri Lanka on April 19 and 20
content: Colombo-based political sources told The Hindu that the visiting Indian Vice President is also scheduled to meet opposition politicians, including those from political parties representing the Tamils of the north and east, as well as the Malaiyaha Tamils from the island’s hill country

title: Kerala dental student death: Case against InstaPay loan app over alleged harassment
content: FIR states agents of the app repeatedly contacted and threatened Nithin Raj for loan repayment, subjecting him to sustained mental harassment and intimidation

title: Trinamool Congress issuing fake press cards to its workers to influence voters: BJP's Suvendu Adhikari
content: Suvendu Adhikari claimed that these individuals were "posing as journalists" to gain access to sensitive locations "to influence voters and manipulate the election process"

title: Trekking SOP announced by Karnataka, online registration and nature guide mandatory
content: A mobile app with details of trekking route maps will be installed in the phone of every trekker, which will help locate a trekker if they get lost

title: More women in the fray, but Tamil Nadu polls remain overwhelmingly male
content: From just 11 women candidates in 1967, accounting for 1.41% of the total number of candidates, the figure has risen to 443 in 2026, making up 11.01% of the contestants. Though the increase is notable, proportionally, women continue to occupy only a small share of the candidate pool

title: Delimitation Bill will not do injustice to any State, says PM Modi in Lok Sabha
content: The Prime Minister said no one should remain under the illusion that they are giving something to the women of this country as reservation in legislatures is their right

title: Delimitation explained: Since 1951, Hindi belt’s Lok Sabha share has fallen more than South's
content: between 1951 and 1977, both the Hindi belt and the southern states saw their share of Lok Sabha seats decline . the main reason was not a gain for the South over the North . but the growing representation of Union Territories and the rising share of western and eastern states .
